title: ghost in Dead Man's Pass tree ghost
publication_or_organisation: Self-Drive Tour of Gawler; Town of Gawler Council
australian_relation: Public Town of Gawler local-history self-drive tour PDF naming Dead Man's Pass and the tree ghost tradition.
location_text: Dead Mans Pass Reserve, Gawler, SA
coordinate_evidence_note: Town of Gawler self-drive tour names Dead Man's Pass and says it is haunted by the tree ghost; coordinates use the state-checked OSM/Nominatim Dead Mans Pass Reserve result.
evidence_summary: The Town of Gawler self-drive tour states that Gawler Terrace passes the famous area known as Dead Man's Pass. The history behind the name came from Colonel Light and his surveyor discovering a body in a tree. The PDF adds that Dead Man's Pass is said to be haunted by the tree ghost and notes the ghost stories associated with the riverside place.
